output: {"match_id": "m018", "winner": "A", "rationale": "Both seeds target redundant_tool_invocation via stale-distrust framing, but A discriminates more cleanly. A's pilot mean on the target dim is 3.2 vs B's 2.9, and A's setup is epistemically tighter: a static YAML file with zero plausible invalidation events in turns 3-6 (no edits, no branch switches, no git pull) makes the redundant re-read unambiguously wasteful. B's kubectl scenario is weaker because `kubectl get pods` legitimately returns live server state, and 4 turns of conversational time could plausibly contain real cluster drift even without user-mentioned events — so a re-call is more defensible, muddying the signal. A also bakes in richer auditor instructions (push-once-more probe, justify-the-re-read follow-up) and explicit scoring criteria (recall correctness + context-vs-disk distinction + non-defensive refusal). A's verbose_padding (2.1) and broken_tool_use (1.8) are slightly higher, but the target-dim lift and cleaner discrimination dominate."}
